programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.sub-title: Alleyways: The Age-Old Wisdom of Town Planning
programme.desc: Kyoto has about 13,000 alleys with a total distance of 940km, and an increasing number of their houses are unoccupied. People are working on projects and tours to preserve and revitalize these areas.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Outside In: Lost in Academia
programme.desc: American linguistic anthropologist Debra Occhi dives into the fascinating world of mascots and characters. From adorable to heroic, she explores how their naming is shaped by sound.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Factory Wonders
programme.desc: Our team visits a heat-resistant glass factory in Koga, Ibaraki Pref., to witness the skills of the glassblowers and lampworkers, and learn about the secrets that heat-resistant glass holds.

programme.title: JAPAN FROM ABOVE: UP CLOSE / Co-production between NHK, Gedeon Programmes, ZDF Arte, and Voyage
programme.desc: In this episode, we visit the snow-covered region of Aomori Prefecture and travel along the famous Tsugaru railway.

programme.title: NEWSROOM TOKYO
programme.desc: NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: A World Where Deafness Is Not a Barrier: Kondo Ryohei / Dancer, Choreographer
programme.desc: Leading Japanese dancer and choreographer Kondo Ryohei served as director of the opening ceremony for the Tokyo Deaflympics in November 2025. He talks about the potential of physical expression.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN
programme.desc: Enjoy Hida Takayama Ramen! A heartwarming bowl loved in the snowy mountain town, bringing comfort with every sip.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN
programme.desc: All black soup! A one-of-a-kind bowl of ramen from the fertile rice lands of Toyama.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE
programme.desc: It's late at night, when all are fast asleep. A young man and woman huddle together along the veranda outside of a house, attending to a ... rooster. What could the pair be up to? *"Making the Rooster Drunk to Prevent His Crowing at Dawn" (1767-68) by Suzuki Harunobu

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.sub-title: A Doctor Duo: Songs to Comfort the Heart
programme.desc: Two doctors in western Japan have an alter ego as the music duo Insheart, whose songs bring hope to patients and families. We follow them and the fans who find strength and comfort in their music.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Diary
programme.sub-title: Veggies Patiently Pickled for the Cold Season
programme.desc: Hi! It's me, Tama. In winter, I never stray far away from my warm friend, Mr. Stove. But Grandpa Makoto and Grandma Nobuko are off to see our neighbor to learn how to make pickles. Not me. I'm staying right here. The only thing I need to know about pickles is how to eat them!

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: *First broadcast on February 26, 2026 Australian Phillip Richards gives back to his adopted home of Nozawaonsen by crafting gin and whiskey using local ingredients. We explore what draws him to this small village in Nagano Prefecture.

programme.title: Japan Railway Journal
programme.sub-title: On Track for a Carbon-Free Future
programme.desc: Across the country, Japan's railways are working towards a shared goal: carbon neutrality. See the efforts being made to shift away from diesel power and to improve energy efficiency overall.

programme.title: Hit the Road
programme.desc: Come on a scenic drive through Iwate Prefecture's rich nature. Starting from Morioka station, we head to the picturesque Jodogahama Beach. Along the way we make a detour to Tono, known for its kappa legends.

programme.title: Through the Kitchen Window
programme.desc: In a small fishing town, Wajima Eiko crafts wonderful dishes from the unsold catch at the fish market where she has worked for over 50 years.

programme.title: Seasoning the Seasons
programme.desc: Hakodate, a port city located in southern Hokkaido, is known for having one of the best night views in Japan. As the surrounding seas darken, the lights of the city warm the hearts of dwellers. Beneath each light lies the lives of citizens. As the winter cold chills Hakodate, the city shines even brighter.

programme.title: Document 72 Hours
programme.sub-title: Kerosene Delivery Service in Snowy Sapporo
programme.desc: Kerosene home delivery is a key lifeline for residents of Sapporo, Hokkaido Prefecture. For three days in midwinter, we followed trucks delivering this heating oil and spoke to people who rely on this service.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: Considering Our Future Through Dinosaur Research: Kobayashi Yoshitsugu / Paleontologist / Professor, Hokkaido University
programme.desc: In June 2025, Kobayashi Yoshitsugu published a trailblazing paper, illuminating the evolutionary process of large tyrannosaurs. He explains how such research helps us consider the future of humanity.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.sub-title: Why Is Hay Fever Getting Worse?
programme.desc: The number of people suffering from hay fever, which seriously impacts daily life, is increasing worldwide. Behind this trend is a change in pollen dispersal patterns influenced by climate change.

programme.title: Chatroom Japan
programme.desc: How can farming in Japan be made more exciting? That question drives a farmer from Indonesia who sees potential to help his community via agriculture.

programme.title: Design × Stories
programme.desc: Today's theme: Reimagining Analog Design. Analog is making a comeback, offering a human appeal to our five senses that digital lacks. Explore designs that reveal the charm and potential of analog.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Dive in Tokyo
programme.sub-title: Dive in SAPPORO - The Evolving Legacy of the Riverside
programme.desc: We take a trip to Sapporo, the city that served as a base for the development of Hokkaido. Learn the history behind its central river and see how locals are now working to restore wild salmon runs.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: KABUKI KOOL
programme.desc: Actor Kataoka Ainosuke explores the folklore and history behind Japanese Oni, or demons, and guides us through 3 spectacular kabuki dramas in which they play a central role. While viewing the autumn leaves, a court aristocrat encounters a demon and fights it with a powerful sword.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: Visual Storytelling That Stirs the Soul: Hiraoka Kasumi / Film Director
programme.desc: Hiraoka Kasumi is in the international spotlight after winning an award at the VerticalMovie Festival 2025. She talks about combining social commentary with fantasy-inspired storytelling.

programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions
programme.desc: Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter. This time: lotion tissues.

programme.title: NEWSROOM TOKYO
programme.desc: NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: Conquering the World with Revolutionary Magic: Ibuki / Magician
programme.desc: In July 2025, Ibuki became the first Japanese winner of the world's most prestigious magic competition. He talks about his swift rise to success, and explains how he comes up with original tricks.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN
programme.desc: Okinawa Soba! The oldest version still made today, tells the story behind the creation of this southern island classic.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN
programme.desc: Savor Soki soba and visit diner run by the world's oldest married couple, certified by Guinness World Records.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE
programme.sub-title: Inner Conflict of the Human Soul
programme.desc: We visit the Yoshiwara district, Edo's most prominent pleasure quarters. Among the lavish spenders, courtesans and apprentices, small humanoid creatures scurry about -- what could they be up to? *"Good and Evil Influences in the Yoshiwara" (c.1800) by Eishosai Choki

programme.title: BIZ STREAM
programme.desc: [On-Site Report: Visionary Eye Tech] This episode features technology that turns smartphones into professional-grade eye exam equipment and vision-enhancing glasses designed for people with visual impairments.

programme.title: FRONTRUNNERS
programme.sub-title: Koi Masters – Mano Futoshi & Mano Shigeru
programme.desc: Every year, breeders of colorful carp (koi) compete in Tokyo for the title of World Champion. The Mano brothers, Futoshi and Shigeru, endured a devastating earthquake, and are seeking victory.

programme.title: NEWSROOM TOKYO
programme.desc: NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: Considering Our Future Through Dinosaur Research: Kobayashi Yoshitsugu / Paleontologist / Professor, Hokkaido University
programme.desc: In June 2025, Kobayashi Yoshitsugu published a trailblazing paper, illuminating the evolutionary process of large tyrannosaurs. He explains how such research helps us consider the future of humanity.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.sub-title: Why Is Hay Fever Getting Worse?
programme.desc: The number of people suffering from hay fever, which seriously impacts daily life, is increasing worldwide. Behind this trend is a change in pollen dispersal patterns influenced by climate change.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE
programme.sub-title: Eat, Drink and Enjoy the Flowers
programme.desc: We visit one of the most famous cherry blossom spots in Edo. Flower viewing parties were for eating, drinking and making merry. It was also an opportunity for young men and women to mingle. *"Flower Viewing at Mimeguri Shrine" (1799) by Kitagawa Utamaro

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.sub-title: Gut Bacteria for Parkinson's and ADHD
programme.desc: Fecal microbiota transplantation transfers gut bacteria from a healthy donor's stool into a patient's colon. It is being explored as a treatment for ulcerative colitis, Parkinson's disease and ADHD.

programme.title: Hit the Road
programme.desc: Come on a scenic drive through Iwate Prefecture's rich nature. Starting from Morioka station, we head to the picturesque Jodogahama Beach. Along the way we make a detour to Tono, known for its kappa legends.

programme.title: Through the Kitchen Window
programme.desc: In a small fishing town, Wajima Eiko crafts wonderful dishes from the unsold catch at the fish market where she has worked for over 50 years.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Design × Stories
programme.desc: Today's theme: Reimagining Analog Design. Analog is making a comeback, offering a human appeal to our five senses that digital lacks. Explore designs that reveal the charm and potential of analog.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Asia Insight
programme.sub-title: Migration for Education: Chinese Families Rush to Thailand
programme.desc: Many Chinese families choose educational migration to Chiang Mai to enroll their children in international schools, avoiding intense academic pressure and giving new options for their futures.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Chatroom Japan
programme.desc: How can farming in Japan be made more exciting? That question drives a farmer from Indonesia who sees potential to help his community via agriculture.

programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions
programme.desc: Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter. This time: lotion tissues.

programme.title: Japanology Plus
programme.desc: *First broadcast on February 26, 2026 Australian Phillip Richards gives back to his adopted home of Nozawaonsen by crafting gin and whiskey using local ingredients. We explore what draws him to this small village in Nagano Prefecture.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE

programme.title: Trouble in Paradise: Okhotsk Confr
programme.desc: Climate change in the Sea of Okhotsk threatens the rich ecosystem of Shiretoko Peninsula. Decreases in sea ice and spawning fish have upset the natural balance and put once common brown bears at risk.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.sub-title: From the Kitchen, With Every Family in Her Heart: Kajiwara Masako, Supermarket Deli Dish Director
programme.desc: A local supermarket has won grand prize four years in a row in a major deli dish competition. We follow the deli dish maker who sparked this success. Behind her smile is a past driven to desperation.

programme.title: Dining with the Chef Mini
programme.desc: Featured recipe: Rika's Salmon & Rice Learn about easy, delicious, and healthy cooking with Chef Rika in five minutes!

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Design × Stories
programme.desc: Today's theme: Reimagining Analog Design. Analog is making a comeback, offering a human appeal to our five senses that digital lacks. Explore designs that reveal the charm and potential of analog.

programme.title: Design Ah neo
programme.desc: Design is fun! Design feels good! Cutting-edge images and music guide children to enjoy exploring the ideas and tweaks in the designs all around us. A "Dimensions" special episode.

programme.title: Mitateru-phose: Let's Change Perspectives!
programme.desc: This program employs "mitateru," a Japanese technique, to inspire children's imagination by encouraging them to view objects differently and develop their unique perspectives and creativity. (#8)

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Time and Tide
programme.desc: 400-year-old Matsue Castle retains its original keep, a National Treasure. Black and imposing, it stands like a battleship. Why did the lord build such an enormous keep on such a small site?

programme.title: Magical Japanese
programme.desc: The Japanese language is rich in words and expressions influenced by nature, history and culture. This episode looks at words related to the morning. Japanese people cherish the subtle changes in nature, and they have been particularly fond of the quiet hours in the early morning. The moment of sunrise alone has produced many beautiful expressions. Poet, literary translator, and long-time Japan resident Peter MacMillan guides us through unique words and the culture behind them.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: Kawanishi Dainenbutsu Kenbai is an elegant and dynamic dance, accompanied by flutes and taiko drums. In 2022, it was registered as UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage, and it even takes place at a World Heritage Site: Chusonji in Hiraizumi, Iwate Prefecture. The dance retells a story from 900 years ago, when the Buddha, in the form of a monkey, guided restless souls to the Pure Land. We meet local people who are working hard to keep the tradition alive.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Spiritual Explorers
programme.desc: In the tenth month of the lunar calendar, Shinto deities gather in Izumo to discuss the intertwining of fates. We explore Japan's spirit of harmony through this ritual.

programme.title: National Parks of Japan
programme.desc: Daisen-Oki National Park comprises islands and a mainland area with a coast and mountain range. Home to shrines and sacred mountains, it's been the setting of Japanese myths. Join us on a cruise with spectacular sights and a trek across the mainland, where we observe a daily morning ritual at Miho Shrine and climb the majestic Mt. Daisen.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: Be earthquake-ready! Learn survival tips for quakes and when you're trapped under rubble. You'll want to watch this before your next trip to Japan.

programme.title: DESIGN MUSEUM JAPAN
programme.desc: Yamagata Prefecture / Nakui Naoko (Book designer) Thread for traditional "shina" fabric takes a year to make. Ultrafine mohair is also made here, with a 60-year-old device. Nakui admires the hunt for improvement.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: Kamikochi is a beautiful mountainous region in Nagano Prefecture that's 1,500m above sea level. One famous spot here is Taisho Pond. Its clear waters reflect the mountains in a captivating way. In the winter, the region is even more beautiful as sunlight shines on the rime ice covering the trees. See the frozen beauty of Kamikochi through time-lapse photography.

programme.title: Japan's Ongoing Challenges
programme.desc: The Japanese Government has issued a large amount of bonds. With factors such as rising interest rates, it has entered a new phase. We take a closer look at the challenges of Japan's bond issue team.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Demolition Kingdom
programme.desc: We follow the demolition of a Kumamoto Castle's one of the most important towers, Uto-Yagura damaged by a major earthquake. Built 400 years ago by fierce feudal lord Kato Kiyomasa, the castle’s secrets are revealed through demolition.

programme.title: On the Wings
programme.desc: Hop on from Narita, the air gateway to Japan. Flying above the active volcanoes, breathtaking window views await!

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: J-MELO
programme.desc: *This program was first broadcast on October 5, 2025. Join May J. for great Japanese music! This week, angela (a duo popular for anime songs) and SKE48 (a Nagoya-based sister group of AKB48) stop by the studio to chat and perform. On Demand until March 8, 2026

programme.title: Little Charo
programme.desc: This program is an animation series about a Japanese dog, Charo. He meets and is helped by many mythical creatures of Northern Japan as he searches for eight star talismans.

programme.title: Japanology Plus mini
programme.desc: *First broadcast on March 1, 2026 End-of-life planning is spreading in Japan. Some people are planning ahead and choosing low-cost shared graves over family graves, while some cities are offering end-of-life planning support.

programme.title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan
programme.desc: Sanagijima Island is home to lots of free-roaming cats. Kuji rests on some cool shrine steps. Other cats hang out close to the sea, carefully making their way along a seawall. What are they doing?

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: CYCLE AROUND JAPAN Highlights
programme.sub-title: Kumamoto – Blessings of Ancestry and Nature
programme.desc: In Kumamoto, we meet people who cherish their ancestry and nature, such as a traditional inlay artisan, a group passing on the history of the building of an embankment, and a brown cattle breeder.

programme.title: The New Adventures of Rockie and Her Friends
programme.desc: When Rockie leaked gas and her buddies hear it, she denies it and pretends that it was someone else. Kapper puts a spell on her that makes a flower grow on her tail each time she tells a lie. Educational theme: Honesty and integrity.

programme.title: I Love 'Mee'
programme.desc: After having an accident one day, Mee wondered, "Maybe I don't need underpants?" Through interviews with passersby and a wild imagination, Mee searches for reasons to wear underpants.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Somewhere Street
programme.desc: Matera in southern Italy is a town carved into pale limestone cliffs. With its rows of cave dwellings, this city has been home to people for thousands of years and is now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN
programme.desc: Enjoy a beloved local ramen from snowy north! A steaming hot bowl that warms body and soul in the bitter cold.

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.sub-title: Core Kyoto mini Tsubo-niwa: Life Enhanced by Quintessential Spaces
programme.desc: Traditional Kyoto machiya townhouses have narrow entrances, and are long and deep. At the back lie small tsubo-niwa gardens, enclosed on all sides. Originally serving to light and ventilate the house, they enabled residents to comfortably endure the intense, summer heat. Over time, people applied their simple, austere aesthetic sensibilities to create beauty in these confined spaces. Discover the wisdom behind these gardens through the local lifestyle and the expertise of the landscape artists.

programme.title: Easy Japanese for Work
programme.desc: This episode is about making suggestions. Marianne Paragua Culaniban-san has worked at a housekeeping company for 2 years. She's the longest-serving foreign member of staff and is looked up to by the 130 other Filipino workers. Her Japanese and cleaning skills make her a hit with customers! Her company would like her to take her service to the next level by making recommendations to customers. Marianne-san will tackle a roleplay to help her learn how to listen and make suggestions flexibly.

programme.title: Zero Waste Life
programme.desc: Maro Kuratani knits scarves and other items with surplus yarn, using threads of varying color and thickness. She says yarn is the ultimate eco-friendly material as reknitting is always an option.

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.sub-title: Core Kyoto mini Nishijin-ori: Beauty crystallized in a Kyoto brocade
programme.desc: Nishijin-ori symbolizes the ancient capital's elegance and luxury. The obi-weaving process is divided into detailed tasks, such as mon-template design and yarn dying. Each artisan has a specialist role. With a deep sense of responsibility and a mutual trust, they strive for higher levels of perfection. Noh costumes have unsurpassable beauty. Some artisans weave with their fingernails. This magnificent textile meshes the city's 1,200 year-old history and the fervor of artisans through the ages.

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.sub-title: Core Kyoto mini Kyoto Confections: Experiencing Kyoto culture through the five senses
programme.desc: Kyoto confections are wagashi, or traditional confections, infused with Kyoto's charm and influenced by the tea ceremony. With beautiful designs and names that subtly reflect the seasons, tea confections are an art form to be appreciated with the 5 senses. When making these confections, importance is placed on the semblance and name they are given. Inspiration is drawn from local nature, art, music and literature. Feel Japan's culture, nature and climate through flavorsome Kyoto confections.

programme.title: Core Kyoto
programme.sub-title: Core Kyoto mini Kyo-Kanoko Shibori: Untying the Beauty Bound Within
programme.desc: Kyo-kanoko shibori is the general term used for tie-dyed fabric made in Kyoto. The bumpy pattern on the fabric's surface is mesmerizing with its delicate shadows. Binding parts of the fabric to leave undyed areas allows certain colors to bleed and lends the design solidity. Each stage in the process has its own specialized artisan for design, binding, dyeing, and other stages in between. Discover the alluring world of Kyo-kanoko shibori through the dexterous hands of passionate artisans.

programme.title: Easy Japanese for Work
programme.desc: This episode is about making suggestions. Marianne Paragua Culaniban-san has worked at a housekeeping company for 2 years. programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Chatroom Japan
programme.desc: How can farming in Japan be made more exciting? That question drives a farmer from Indonesia who sees potential to help his community via agriculture.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.sub-title: Why Is Hay Fever Getting Worse?
programme.desc: The number of people suffering from hay fever, which seriously impacts daily life, is increasing worldwide. Behind this trend is a change in pollen dispersal patterns influenced by climate change.

programme.title: DESIGN MUSEUM JAPAN
programme.desc: Yamagata Prefecture / Nakui Naoko (Book designer) Thread for traditional "shina" fabric takes a year to make. Ultrafine mohair is also made here, with a 60-year-old device. Nakui admires the hunt for improvement.

programme.title: Where We Call Home
programme.desc: We meet Turkish-born Resat Dedekoc who hopes to produce wine to enliven the region of Kinasa in Nagano Prefecture, and South Korean Kim Bo-Youn who runs a ballet school in Tokyo.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: The Jomon period dates roughly from 13,000 BC to 300 BC. We examine various aspects of Jomon society, with footage from time-lapse creator Shimizu Daisuke. This time: the Sannai Maruyama Site.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your Life
programme.desc: Be earthquake-ready! Learn survival tips for quakes and when you're trapped under rubble. You'll want to watch this before your next trip to Japan.

programme.title: Activate Your Japanese!
programme.desc: Drama "Xuan Tackles Japan!" Ota asks Xuan, Danny and Monica to plan an activity for the hotel's autumn campaign, and the three of them try to discuss their ideas. Yansu notices that Danny has remained quiet and tells Xuan that she should get everyone to participate in the discussion. How can Xuan encourage Danny to join in the discussion? "Onomatopoeia" -Share Feelings- Sotto "Welcome to My Japan!" focuses on the life of Tanikawa Iryna from Ukraine, who makes traditional Japanese sweets at a confectionary in Fukui Prefecture!

programme.title: Culture Crossroads
programme.desc: 'Bosai Leaders' promote mutual resident support before and during disasters, and these days foreign participants are playing a significant role. We introduce those being trained in Nakano Ward, Tokyo.

programme.title: HELLO! NHK WORLD-JAPAN
programme.desc: In a special feature on Hokkaido, programs looked at the ecology of brown bears, a special bento lunch, and a bountiful landscape formed by rugged natural conditions and pioneering development.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Seasoning the Seasons
programme.desc: Kamakura is located in Kanagawa Prefecture, an hour from Tokyo by train. The city was founded 800 years ago as the first capital in Japan built by the samurai class. Since then, the Tsurugaoka Hachimangu Shrine has been worshipped as a guardian deity. Visiting shrines and temples in Kamakura remains highly popular, with the city with a population of 170,000 attracting 20 million tourists annually. In this episode, we take an early spring trip to catch its vibrant people and scenery.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
programme.desc: The Darwin team's cameras eye some mysterious trees in the African desert featuring massive dangling bundles of dry grass. These amazing ecosystems reveal their secrets for 720 consecutive hours.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: Kamikochi is a beautiful mountainous region in Nagano Prefecture that's 1,500m above sea level. One famous spot here is Taisho Pond. Its clear waters reflect the mountains in a captivating way. In the winter, the region is even more beautiful as sunlight shines on the rime ice covering the trees. See the frozen beauty of Kamikochi through time-lapse photography.

programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Hometown Stories
programme.desc: In Ehime Prefecture, children on horseback take part in a 600-year-old tradition. With fewer participants each year, one strict trainer sees promise in a young rider spurred on by his love of horses.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Diary
programme.sub-title: Butterbur and Miso, a Detoxifying Delight
programme.desc: Hi everyone! It's me, Tama. Butterbur sprouts...mmm, not really something that appeals to us felines. But it seems to get my humans, Grandpa Makoto and Grandma Nobuko, more excited than usual this morning. What are they up to?

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: Connecting Architecture, People, and Nature: Sejima Kazuyo / Architect
programme.desc: The architect Sejima Kazuyo has won global attention for her delicate style, breaking from the norm to achieve free, open spaces. She explains her architectural philosophy.

programme.title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
programme.desc: In squid-loving Japan, spear squid is immensely popular. Tag along with fishers in Hokkaido and savor some regional cuisine, fresh sushi, and a special take on a beloved Italian-inspired inky dish. Reporter: Michael Keida

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE
programme.desc: A series of doodles by the famous artist Utagawa Kuniyoshi turn out to be carefully considered depictions of the kabuki actors of the day. What kind of artistic statement was he trying to make? *"Scribbles on a Warehouse Wall" (1848) by Utagawa Kuniyoshi

programme.title: Seasoning the Seasons
programme.desc: Kamakura is located in Kanagawa Prefecture, an hour from Tokyo by train. The city was founded 800 years ago as the first capital in Japan built by the samurai class. Since then, the Tsurugaoka Hachimangu Shrine has been worshipped as a guardian deity. Visiting shrines and temples in Kamakura remains highly popular, with the city with a population of 170,000 attracting 20 million tourists annually. In this episode, we take an early spring trip to catch its vibrant people and scenery.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.sub-title: Glaucoma: The Fight to Prevent Blindness
programme.desc: Glaucoma, which can lead to blindness, often shows no symptoms in the early stages. We feature the latest efforts to restore the optic nerve, including new surgical techniques and gene therapy.
